maxContractTerm: No contract for pupil transportation between an Illinois public school district and a private contractor — inclusive of any proposed renewals — may exceed 10 years. The statute reads in pertinent part: "Subject to Section 6-106.11 of the Illinois Vehicle Code, school boards may enter into contracts for any period of time deemed appropriate by those school boards for transportation of pupils to and from school; however, no contract, inclusive of any proposed renewals, may exceed 10 years. All contracts for a period of time greater than 5 years that do not include the use of electric vehicles for pupil transportation shall include a termination option after 5 years. Nothing in this Section prohibits contract opener clauses for any purpose from being included in the contract. A contract for pupil transportation that utilizes a significant percentage of electric vehicles may be entered into by a school board for up to 15 years if the contract relies on capital or infrastructure purchases or improvements that cannot reasonably be justified in a shorter-term contract." 105 ILCS 5/29-6.1 (Source: P.A. 103-430, eff. 1-1-24). Practical structure: (1) any awarded term up to 10 years (initial term + any renewals counted together) using non-EV equipment; (2) if a non-EV term exceeds 5 years, the contract must contain a 5-year termination option (effectively "5 years + up to 5 more, with a 5-year termination option"); (3) up to 15 years permitted only for contracts using "a significant percentage of electric vehicles" where the longer term is justified by capital or infrastructure investment. 105 ILCS 5/29-6.1 is expressly "subject to Section 6-106.11 of the Illinois Vehicle Code" (625 ILCS 5/6-106.11), which governs school-bus-driver permits and is not a term-or-renewal rule. No separate cap appears in the Illinois Administrative Code (23 Ill. Adm. Code 1.510 covers safety/operations only). Official URL: https://www.ilga.gov/documents/legislation/ilcs/documents/010500050K29-6.1.htm
rebidRequired: Conditional. Illinois does not impose an automatic re-bid at the expiration of a pupil-transportation contract term. Under 105 ILCS 5/29-6.1, a contract that has run for less than 10 years may be replaced or renewed by the board without an automatic competitive resolicitation, so long as the cumulative term (initial + renewals) does not exceed 10 years (15 years for qualifying EV contracts). Two statutory triggers convert "no automatic re-bid" into a required competitive process: (i) Award of a NEW pupil-transportation contract is governed by 105 ILCS 5/10-20.21(a)(xvi), which exempts pupil-transportation contracts from the lowest-responsible-bidder rule but still requires that such contracts "be advertised in the same manner as competitive bids and awarded by first considering the bidder or bidders most able to provide safety and comfort for the pupils, stability of service, and any other factors set forth in the request for proposal regarding quality of service, and then price"; and (ii) When a district self-operates buses (i.e., no contract is in place), 105 ILCS 5/29-6.4 requires the board to "solicit sealed bids" upon timely written request from an interested private bus contractor, after which the board may either "elect to enter into a contract as provided in Section 29-6.1 with the lowest responsible bidder for transportation" or continue self-operating (a request need not be entertained more than once every 2 years, and is untimely if made within 3 months of, or more than 24 months before, the expiration of any applicable collective-bargaining agreement). The standing limitation on bid-award challenges is statutory: "at no time shall a cause of action lie against a school board for awarding a pupil transportation contract per the standards set forth in this subsection (a) unless the cause of action is based on fraudulent conduct." 105 ILCS 5/10-20.21(a). Official URLs: https://www.ilga.gov/documents/legislation/ilcs/documents/010500050K10-20.21.htm and https://www.ilga.gov/documents/legislation/ilcs/documents/010500050K29-6.4.htm
renewalMechanics: Permitted within the 10-year aggregate cap (15-year cap for qualifying EV contracts). 105 ILCS 5/29-6.1 grants school boards open-ended structural discretion ("any period of time deemed appropriate by those school boards") subject to three express limits: (a) the cumulative term "inclusive of any proposed renewals" cannot exceed 10 years; (b) any non-EV term longer than 5 years "shall include a termination option after 5 years"; and (c) "Nothing in this Section prohibits contract opener clauses for any purpose from being included in the contract." Renewals therefore may be effected by mutual written agreement of the parties and board action, in any combination (e.g., 5 + 5, 3 + 3 + 4, 10-year initial with annual openers, etc.), so long as the aggregate of the initial award and all renewals does not exceed 10 years for non-EV contracts (15 years for qualifying EV contracts). The statute does not itself prescribe CPI/index adjustments, fuel escalators, or automatic evergreen renewal; these are matters left to contract drafting and are commonly included via the "contract opener clauses for any purpose" authority recognized in 29-6.1. No state-agency approval is required to renew. Note: the prior pre-2024 version of 29-6.1 (which capped contracts at 3 years with up to two 1-year extensions, then year-to-year subject to a competing bidder's request) was repealed and replaced by P.A. 103-430 effective January 1, 2024; that older year-to-year rebid-trigger language no longer appears in the statute. Official URL: https://www.ilga.gov/documents/legislation/ilcs/documents/010500050K29-6.1.htm
statutoryCitation: Primary transportation-specific authority: 105 ILCS 5/29-6.1 (Contracts for transportation), Illinois School Code, Article 29 — Source: P.A. 103-430, eff. 1-1-24. Official text: https://www.ilga.gov/documents/legislation/ilcs/documents/010500050K29-6.1.htm. Cross-reference incorporated by 29-6.1: 625 ILCS 5/6-106.11 (Illinois Vehicle Code — school bus driver permits required of contractors) — official text: https://www.ilga.gov/documents/legislation/ilcs/documents/062500050K6-106.11.htm. General school-district procurement authority that reaches pupil transportation by express carve-out: 105 ILCS 5/10-20.21(a)(xvi) (pupil-transportation contracts must be "advertised in the same manner as competitive bids and awarded by first considering the bidder or bidders most able to provide safety and comfort for the pupils, stability of service, and any other factors set forth in the request for proposal regarding quality of service, and then price") — Source: P.A. 102-1101, eff. 6-29-22; 103-8, eff. 1-1-24. Official text: https://www.ilga.gov/documents/legislation/ilcs/documents/010500050K10-20.21.htm. Companion rebid-trigger provision (applicable where the district self-operates and a private contractor makes a timely written request): 105 ILCS 5/29-6.4 (Non-contract transportation; bids; reimbursement) — Source: P.A. 93-953, eff. 1-1-05. Official text (verified): https://www.ilga.gov/documents/legislation/ilcs/documents/010500050K29-6.4.htm. ISBE administrative rule on pupil transportation (operations/safety only — not procurement or term/renewal): 23 Ill. Adm. Code 1.510 — JCAR official text: http://www.ilga.gov/commission/jcar/admincode/023/023000010E05100R.html.
districtVariance: Yes. Although the controlling statute (105 ILCS 5/29-6.1, P.A. 103-430, eff. 1-1-24) is uniform statewide, two distinct sources of practical interpretive variance exist: (1) Stale ISBE guidance vs. current statute. The ISBE "Pupil Transportation Frequently Asked Questions" (https://www.isbe.net/Documents/pupil-transp-faq.pdf) still answers the FAQ on transportation-contract length using the OLD pre-2024 statutory language ("school boards may enter into contracts for up to three years … extended for up to two additional years by agreement of the parties, and thereafter may be extended on a year-to-year basis … no such contract may be extended on a year-to-year basis if a school board receives a timely request from another interested contractor that a contract be let by bid"), even though that language was repealed and replaced by P.A. 103-430 effective January 1, 2024. The statute (10-year cap, 5-year termination option, 15-year EV exception) controls; the FAQ is not authority and is out of date. District officials relying on the ISBE FAQ rather than the statute could understate the lawful maximum term by 5 years (or 12 years in the EV case). (2) Wide variation in initial-term/option structures used in district RFPs and contracts. Review of district-issued transportation RFPs and contracts (e.g., Township High School District 227 "Three Year Contract with an option for a one-year extension"; Rockford BOE "three (3) years" initial term per 29-6.1; CCSD46 and Champaign-area districts using 5+5 or 3+2+5 structures; multiple districts using "up to seven (7) additional years by mutual agreement of the parties, pursuant to 105 ILCS 5/29-6.1") shows that within the 10-year statutory cap, the chosen term length, renewal mechanism, and inclusion/scope of CPI, fuel, and contract-opener clauses vary substantially from district to district. The Illinois Association of School Boards (Update, "Navigating the Evolving Map of School Purchasing") notes the law allows "the length of a student transportation contracts to be up to ten years before going out to re-bid," confirming that districts may but need not use the full term. The substantive statewide rule is uniform; the practical contract architecture is not.
specialEducation: No different state-law term limit, rebid requirement, procurement rule, or renewal mechanic applies to special-education, IEP-mandated, McKinney-Vento (homeless), foster-care, or alternative student transportation contracts in Illinois at the contract-formation level. 105 ILCS 5/29-6.1 speaks to "contracts … for transportation of pupils to and from school" without distinction by student population; 105 ILCS 5/10-20.21(a)(xvi)'s pupil-transportation procurement carve-out likewise applies generally to "contracts providing for the transportation of pupils." 105 ILCS 5/29-6.4 (rebid trigger on district self-operation) expressly extends to special-education cooperatives ("A district or special education cooperative is not required to respond to such a request more than once every 2 years"), indicating that the same competitive-bidding framework applies to special-education transportation contracts; it does not impose a stricter or looser rule. Illinois Article 14 (Children with Disabilities) and related ISBE special-education regulations require districts to provide special transportation as part of FAPE/IEP services and govern reimbursement under 105 ILCS 5/14-7 series (verified that 14-7.02 contains no special procurement/term/rebid/renewal rule for special-education transportation), but they do not prescribe a different contract term, rebid frequency, or renewal mechanic. McKinney-Vento (42 U.S.C. § 11431 et seq.) and IDEA (20 U.S.C. § 1400 et seq.) impose federal duties on the district to transport, but federal law does not displace the Illinois state-law contracting framework (term/rebid/renewal); the duty to transport is a substantive obligation, while the procurement vehicle remains 29-6.1 / 10-20.21(a)(xvi). Practical observation, not state law: many Illinois district RFPs separate regular and special-education routes into either separate awards or separate line items within a single 29-6.1 contract, reflecting operational differences (van/bus types, monitor/aide staffing, individualized routing) rather than any statutory differentiation in term, rebid, or renewal.
notesAndSpecialProvisions:
- **Small-purchase / dollar-threshold exemptions (105 ILCS 5/10-20.21(a)).** The $35,000 sealed-bid/public-advertisement threshold (or a lower amount fixed by board policy) applies to school-district contracts generally, but the pupil-transportation carve-out at 10-20.21(a)(xvi) substitutes a sealed-RFP framework keyed to safety/comfort/stability/quality first and price second regardless of dollar amount once the district elects to contract for service. (Source: https://www.ilga.gov/documents/legislation/ilcs/documents/010500050K10-20.21.htm.)
- **Sole-source / emergency procurement (105 ILCS 5/10-20.21(a)).** Statutory exceptions to the bid requirement include emergency expenditures approved by 3/4 of the board (item (xiv)) and goods/services procurable from only one source (item (xiii)); these general carve-outs reach pupil transportation only in true sole-source or genuine-emergency circumstances and do not override 29-6.1's term limits or 10-20.21(a)(xvi)'s safety-and-quality-first standard for transportation contracting.
- **Cooperative purchasing / piggyback authority.**
  - **Governmental Joint Purchasing Act (30 ILCS 525/).** School districts (as taxing public entities / governmental units) are eligible to make joint purchases of supplies and services, including transportation services, with one or more other governmental units, subject to competitive solicitation conducted by one of the participating units; the Act expressly supersedes inconsistent procurement provisions when joint authority is exercised. Official Act page: https://www.ilga.gov/legislation/ilcs/ilcs5.asp?ActID=537.
  - **Education Purchasing Program (105 ILCS 5/Article 28A and references in 10-20.21).** ISBE-designated "State master contracts" and "certified education purchasing contracts" are explicitly recognized in 10-20.21 as alternatives to a district's own sealed-bid procurement; State master contracts are "not subject to the requirements of this paragraph." A district may "review and consider as a bid under subsection (a) of this Section certified education purchasing contracts that are already available through the State education purchasing entity." (Source: https://www.ilga.gov/documents/legislation/ilcs/documents/010500050K10-20.21.htm.)
  - **District-to-district contracts (105 ILCS 5/29-6).** "Any school district, including any non-high school district, may contract at actual cost with 1 or more school districts for the transportation of pupils to and from the school attended" (P.A. 78-1245) — a recognized alternative to procuring from a private contractor.
- **Mandatory rebid trigger when district self-operates (105 ILCS 5/29-6.4).** A board operating its own bus fleet must solicit sealed bids on receipt of a "timely" certified-mail request from a private contractor; the board may then either contract with the lowest responsible bidder pursuant to 29-6.1 or continue self-operating after publicly disclosing the bid amounts and the district's fully allocated costs at a regular board meeting within 30 days. Districts and special-education cooperatives need not entertain such a request more than once every 2 years, and a request is untimely if made within 3 months of, or more than 24 months before, the expiration of an applicable collective-bargaining agreement.
- **Charter schools.** Illinois charter schools operate under Article 27A of the School Code (105 ILCS 5/27A-1 et seq.) and their charters; charter schools generally are not subject to the School Code procurement and transportation-contracting provisions in the same manner as traditional districts unless their charter so provides. The 29-6.1 / 10-20.21(a)(xvi) framework controls traditional-district contracting with private bus operators; charter-school contracting practice varies by charter.
- **Special-education differences (operational, not statutory).** No different state-law term/rebid/renewal rule applies. 29-6.4 expressly references "special education cooperatives" alongside districts, confirming uniform application of the rebid trigger. (See specialEducation field.)
- **State-approval requirements.** No state-agency pre-approval is required for a district to enter into, renew, or extend a pupil-transportation contract within the 29-6.1 framework. ISBE approval is required for transportation-reimbursement claims (105 ILCS 5/29-5 and 23 Ill. Adm. Code Part 120 series), not for contract formation.
- **Cross-cutting contract terms required or recognized by 29-6.1.** (a) "Termination option after 5 years" is mandatory for non-EV contracts longer than 5 years. (b) "Contract opener clauses for any purpose" are expressly permitted (e.g., fuel, CPI, regulatory-change openers). (c) EV-utilizing contracts may go to 15 years only if "the contract relies on capital or infrastructure purchases or improvements that cannot reasonably be justified in a shorter-term contract."
- **Anti-challenge shield (105 ILCS 5/10-20.21(a)).** "At no time shall a cause of action lie against a school board for awarding a pupil transportation contract per the standards set forth in this subsection (a) unless the cause of action is based on fraudulent conduct." This sharply limits losing-bidder protests of pupil-transportation award decisions.
- **Stale ISBE guidance.** ISBE's published "Pupil Transportation FAQs" answer to the contract-length FAQ still recites the pre-2024 statutory text. The statute controls; the FAQ is not authority and reads the prior version of 29-6.1. (Identified during adversarial review and noted in District Variance.)
- **Driver-permit / contractor-conduct overlay (625 ILCS 5/6-106.11).** A contracted operator must employ drivers holding a valid School Bus Driver Permit, supply driver names and permit copies to the district, notify the district within 2 business days of any permit suspension or cancellation, refrain from using salvage/junk-status vehicles, and prohibit smoking in contract vehicles. Violations are a business offense (first offense fine $1,000–$10,000) with escalating penalties on repeat violation.
exceptionsUnresolved: All five fields resolved on primary authority. No unresolved field after adversarial validation.

sourceLedgerRows.citation: 105 ILCS 5/29-6.1 (Contracts for transportation) — Source: P.A. 103-430, eff. 1-1-24
sourceLedgerRows.codeCurrency: Re-opened on official ilga.gov on 2026-06-23; source line at this URL is P.A. 103-430, eff. 1-1-24. No later amending Public Act identified in 104th GA bill/PA searches that re-amends 29-6.1 itself.
sourceLedgerRows.fieldsSupported: Max Contract Term; Renewal Mechanics; Statutory Citation; Special Education
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: "Subject to Section 6-106.11 of the Illinois Vehicle Code, school boards may enter into contracts for any period of time deemed appropriate by those school boards for transportation of pupils to and from school; however, no contract, inclusive of any proposed renewals, may exceed 10 years. All contracts for a period of time greater than 5 years that do not include the use of electric vehicles for pupil transportation shall include a termination option after 5 years. Nothing in this Section prohibits contract opener clauses for any purpose from being included in the contract. A contract for pupil transportation that utilizes a significant percentage of electric vehicles may be entered into by a school board for up to 15 years if the contract relies on capital or infrastructure purchases or improvements that cannot reasonably be justified in a shorter-term contract." (Source: P.A. 103-430, eff. 1-1-24.)

sourceLedgerRows.codeCurrency: Re-opened on official ilga.gov on 2026-06-23; source line: P.A. 102-1101, eff. 6-29-22; 103-8, eff. 1-1-24.
sourceLedgerRows.fieldsSupported: Rebid Required at Term-End?; Statutory Citation; Notes (procurement framework, cooperative purchasing, anti-challenge shield)
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: (a)(xvi): "contracts providing for the transportation of pupils, which contracts must be advertised in the same manner as competitive bids and awarded by first considering the bidder or bidders most able to provide safety and comfort for the pupils, stability of service, and any other factors set forth in the request for proposal regarding quality of service, and then price". Closing language: "at no time shall a cause of action lie against a school board for awarding a pupil transportation contract per the standards set forth in this subsection (a) unless the cause of action is based on fraudulent conduct." Recognizes review of "certified education purchasing contracts" (cooperative purchasing).

sourceLedgerRows.citation: 105 ILCS 5/29-6.4 (Non-contract transportation; bids; reimbursement) — Source: P.A. 93-953, eff. 1-1-05
sourceLedgerRows.codeCurrency: Re-opened on official ilga.gov on 2026-06-23; source line: P.A. 93-953, eff. 1-1-05. URL CORRECTED during adversarial pass: the previously-cited fulltext.asp URL returned 404; the .htm canonical URL is the verified working official URL.
sourceLedgerRows.fieldsSupported: Rebid Required at Term-End? (when district self-operates); Special Education (express reference to special-education cooperatives)
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: Requires a school board that provides its own transportation to "solicit sealed bids" upon timely written request from a private contractor; the board may then "elect to enter into a contract as provided in Section 29-6.1 with the lowest responsible bidder for transportation" or continue current system after publicly disclosing fully allocated costs and bid amounts at a regular board meeting within 30 days. "A district or special education cooperative is not required to respond to such a request more than once every 2 years." A request is untimely if made more than 24 months or less than 3 months before the expiration of the collective bargaining or other agreement.

sourceLedgerRows.citation: 625 ILCS 5/6-106.11 (Illinois Vehicle Code — School bus driver permits / contractor obligations) — Source: P.A. 97-224, eff. 7-28-11 (and parallel P.A. 97-229, eff. 7-28-11)
sourceLedgerRows.codeCurrency: Re-opened on official ilga.gov on 2026-06-23; source line: P.A. 97-224 / 97-229, eff. 7-28-11.
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: Governs contractor obligations re: school-bus-driver permits, driver-qualification reporting (provide names + permit copies; notify district within 2 business days of suspension/cancellation), vehicle restrictions (no prior salvage/junk-status vehicles; no smoking in contract vehicles), and escalating fines for violations (first offense business offense, $1,000–$10,000); not a term/renewal/rebid provision. 29-6.1's prefatory clause makes the contract authority "Subject to Section 6-106.11 of the Illinois Vehicle Code."

sourceLedgerRows.citation: 105 ILCS 5/29-6 (Inter-district contracts for transportation) — Source: P.A. 78-1245
sourceLedgerRows.codeCurrency: Re-opened on official ilga.gov on 2026-06-23; source line: P.A. 78-1245.
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: "Any school district, including any non-high school district, may contract at actual cost with 1 or more school districts for the transportation of pupils to and from the school attended." Provides an alternative to private contracting and is distinct from 29-6.1.

sourceLedgerRows.codeCurrency: Act is current through ILGA-published ILCS. URL CORRECTED during adversarial pass — the previously-cited ilcs3.asp URL with ChapterID=7 returned 404; the ilcs5.asp form (ActID=537) is the working canonical Act page (corroborated via WebSearch hit to the official ilga.gov ActID=537 listing and Justia mirror).
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: Authorizes joint purchases of supplies and services among governmental units (including school districts) subject to competitive solicitation conducted by one of the participating units; supersedes inconsistent procurement provisions when joint authority is exercised. Purchases follow Illinois Procurement Code competitive-solicitation procedures and comply with the Local Government Prompt Payment Act.

sourceLedgerRows.sourceTier: 2 - State administrative code / ISBE regulation (official JCAR)
sourceLedgerRows.codeCurrency: Re-opened on JCAR on 2026-06-23; current per JCAR publication. Addresses operational safety only.
sourceLedgerRows.fieldsSupported: Material to documenting administrative-code silence on procurement and term/renewal — supports the conclusion that contract term, rebid, and renewal are governed by statute, not by ISBE rule.
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: Establishes "minimum statewide requirements for operating a school bus," including driver conduct, passenger safety, loading procedures, and accident reporting. Does NOT address procurement, bidding, contract term, renewal/extension, special-education contracting, or cooperative purchasing.

sourceLedgerRows.citation: Public Act 103-430 (103rd General Assembly) — amending 105 ILCS 5/29-6.1, eff. 1-1-24
sourceLedgerRows.codeCurrency: Re-opened on official ilga.gov on 2026-06-23. The act amends 105 ILCS 5/29-6.1 and produces the current 10-year cap, 5-year termination option, and 15-year EV exception. Effective January 1, 2024 (per the ILCS source line at 29-6.1).
sourceLedgerRows.fieldsSupported: Max Contract Term; Renewal Mechanics; District Variance (establishes the current 10-year / 5-year-termination-option / 15-year-EV regime that supersedes the prior 3+2+annual rule).
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: "The School Code is amended by changing Section 29-6.1"; verbatim amending text matches the current section text (10-year cap; "termination option after 5 years" for non-EV >5-year contracts; 15-year EV exception conditioned on capital/infrastructure justification).

Contains no procurement requirements, bidding mandates, or contract renewal specifications — confirming Article 14 does not displace 29-6.1 / 10-20.21(a)(xvi) at the contract-formation level.

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Max Contract Term — 10 years (with 5-year termination option for non-EV >5-year contracts; 15 years for qualifying EV contracts)
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: A later 104th-GA amendment, separate procurement-code carve-out, grandfathering provision, or "notwithstanding/may renew/extension/option/successive/multi-year/not exceed" carve-out elsewhere in the School Code, Procurement Code, or administrative code authorizes a longer aggregate term or undoes the 10-year cap.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: WebSearch: "'105 ILCS 5/29-6.1' amended 104th General Assembly 2025 OR 2026 public act"; WebSearch: "'105 ILCS 5/29-6.1' '104th' amendment OR amended 2025"; direct WebFetch on 29-6.1 official URL; direct WebFetch on PA 103-430; WebFetch on 23 IAC 1.510; review of Article 29 sections for separate term carve-outs.
challengeMatrixRows.result: No disconfirming authority surfaced. ILGA 29-6.1 source line still reads P.A. 103-430, eff. 1-1-24. 104th-GA bill/PA searches surfaced no amendment of 29-6.1. 23 IAC 1.510 addresses safety only. Article 14 (special ed) contains no different term rule.

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Rebid Required at Term-End — Conditional (no automatic rebid; competitive RFP required for new awards under 10-20.21(a)(xvi); 29-6.4 sealed-bid trigger applies when district self-operates)
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: Mandatory rebid at term-end exists in school, procurement, public-contract, or district-purchasing law; or, conversely, broad sole-source/emergency/cooperative-purchasing/board-waiver/negotiated-procurement/small-purchase-threshold exceptions broadly avoid the 10-20.21(a)(xvi) framework.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: WebFetch on 10-20.21 to re-verify item (xvi) and the anti-challenge clause; WebFetch on 29-6.4 to confirm the self-operation rebid trigger; WebSearch for cooperative purchasing under 30 ILCS 525; review of 10-20.21(a)(xiii)-(xiv) for emergency/sole-source carve-outs.
challengeMatrixRows.result: Confirmed. 10-20.21(a)(xvi) governs new-award competitive process for pupil transportation but does not impose automatic re-bid at end of term; 29-6.4 fires only when a district self-operates and a private contractor makes a timely written request. Joint Purchasing Act (30 ILCS 525) and Article 28A education-purchasing-contract recognition operate as alternative procurement vehicles, not as automatic-rebid triggers.

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Renewal Mechanics — permitted within 10-year aggregate cap (15-year EV cap); board action; contract-opener clauses for any purpose expressly authorized; no state-agency approval required
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: Statute or administrative code limits renewals (CPI cap, fuel-adjustment cap, board/state approval requirement, annual-appropriations contingency, public-hearing requirement, mandatory termination notice, capped number of renewals) beyond the 10-year aggregate cap.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: WebFetch on 29-6.1; WebFetch on 10-20.21; WebFetch on 23 IAC 1.510; review of Article 29 for additional renewal-condition provisions.
challengeMatrixRows.result: Confirmed. 29-6.1 imposes three express limits (10-year aggregate cap; 5-year termination option for non-EV >5-year contracts; 15-year cap for EV with capital justification) and otherwise leaves "any period of time deemed appropriate" to the board, expressly allowing "contract opener clauses for any purpose." 23 IAC 1.510 imposes no renewal conditions. No state-agency approval is required.

challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: One or more cited URLs is not the official source, will not re-open, or does not say what Stage 1 claimed; or a different controlling statute/code section exists.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: WebFetch on each cited URL; cross-checked anti-challenge text, EV provisions, and 29-6.4 special-ed-cooperative reference verbatim.
challengeMatrixRows.result: Partially confirmed with two URL corrections. 29-6.1, 10-20.21, 29-6, 625 ILCS 5/6-106.11, 23 IAC 1.510, and PA 103-430 all re-opened on official URLs and matched Stage 1 verbatim claims. 29-6.4 fulltext.asp URL returned 404 — replaced in this pack with the verified .htm canonical URL (https://www.ilga.gov/documents/legislation/ilcs/documents/010500050K29-6.4.htm). 30 ILCS 525 ilcs3.asp URL (ChapterID=7) returned 404 — replaced with the working ilcs5.asp ActID=537 URL. No substantive rule changed; both substitutions are official-source-to-official-source.
challengeMatrixRows.confidenceImpact: No downgrade — citations confirmed at official sources after URL corrections.

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Special Education — same state-law term/rebid/renewal rule applies; no separate Article 14 / IEP / McKinney-Vento / foster-care / homeless / disability transportation contracting carve-out
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: Article 14 (Children with Disabilities), 14-7.02 series, 14-13.01 (special-ed cooperatives), McKinney-Vento implementing rules, or IDEA-implementing state rules impose a different contract term, rebid frequency, or renewal mechanic for special-education or homeless-student transportation.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: WebFetch on 105 ILCS 5/14-7.02; WebSearch: "Illinois special education transportation contract procurement statute '105 ILCS 5/14' different term limit"; review of 29-6.4 special-ed-cooperative reference; review of 29-6.1 for any pupil-population carve-out.
challengeMatrixRows.result: Confirmed. 14-7.02 contains no special procurement/term/rebid/renewal rule for special-ed transportation. 29-6.4 expressly extends the same rebid trigger to special-education cooperatives. 29-6.1 and 10-20.21(a)(xvi) speak generically to "transportation of pupils" without student-population distinction.

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: District Variance — Yes (stale ISBE FAQ vs. current statute; wide variation in district-RFP term/option structures within the 10-year cap)
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: ISBE has issued updated guidance superseding the stale FAQ; district practice is uniform statewide; the variance description is overstated or unsupported.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: WebFetch on the IASB Update article; WebSearch for District 227 / Rockford / CCSD46 transportation RFPs citing 29-6.1; WebFetch on the ISBE FAQ PDF (binary returned).
challengeMatrixRows.result: Confirmed. IASB Update expressly states the GA "amended the statutory language to allow the length of a student transportation contracts to be up to ten years before going out to re-bid," corroborating the new 10-year ceiling. No updated ISBE FAQ surfaced; Stage 1's observation that ISBE's FAQ recites the pre-2024 language is retained as a recorded observation (the FAQ is non-authority). District-RFP variance is illustrative only and not relied upon to establish statewide law.
challengeMatrixRows.confidenceImpact: No downgrade — finding confirmed. Note: the ISBE FAQ component is illustrative of stale-guidance variance, not authority.

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Currentness — 29-6.1 has not been amended since P.A. 103-430 (eff. 1-1-24)
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: A 104th GA Public Act amended 29-6.1 between 1-1-24 and 2026-06-23.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: WebSearch: "'105 ILCS 5/29-6.1' amended 104th General Assembly 2025 OR 2026 public act"; WebSearch: "'105 ILCS 5/29-6.1' '104th' amendment OR amended 2025".
challengeMatrixRows.result: Confirmed. No 104th-GA amendment to 29-6.1 located. The 104th-GA activity touching Article 29 found in Stage 1 (SB1657) amends 29-5, not 29-6.1.
challengeMatrixRows.confidenceImpact: No downgrade — currentness confirmed as of 2026-06-23.

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Applicability boundary — 105 ILCS 5/29-6.1 (school-specific) is the controlling term/renewal rule; general procurement (Procurement Code, Joint Purchasing Act) does not impose a different term/renewal cap on pupil-transportation contracts; charter schools are not subject to 29-6.1 in the same manner as traditional districts unless their charter so provides.
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: A general procurement statute (Illinois Procurement Code, Local Government Joint Purchasing Act, Article 28A education-purchasing program) imposes a stricter or looser term/renewal cap that overrides 29-6.1; charter schools are bound by 29-6.1.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: WebSearch on 30 ILCS 525 in the school-transportation context; review of 10-20.21(a)(xv)-(xvi) recognition of certified education purchasing contracts; review of Article 27A for charter-school procurement carve-outs.
challengeMatrixRows.result: Confirmed. 30 ILCS 525 governs the joint-purchasing vehicle (competitive solicitation by a participating unit; Illinois Procurement Code procedures; Local Government Prompt Payment Act) but does not impose a different aggregate term cap on pupil-transportation contracts; 10-20.21 recognizes certified education purchasing contracts as an alternative procurement vehicle. Charter schools operate under Article 27A and their charters — the 29-6.1 / 10-20.21(a)(xvi) framework is not automatically applied to them.

perFieldVerdicts.note: Re-opened 105 ILCS 5/29-6.1 on official ilga.gov on 2026-06-23. Verbatim text matches Stage 1 quote: 10-year aggregate cap (initial + renewals), mandatory 5-year termination option for non-EV contracts >5 years, 15-year cap for EV contracts justified by capital/infrastructure investment. No 104th-GA amendment surfaced.

Re-opened 105 ILCS 5/10-20.21 and 29-6.4 on official ilga.gov on 2026-06-23. 10-20.21(a)(xvi) competitive-RFP framework for new awards confirmed; 29-6.4 self-operation sealed-bid trigger confirmed; anti-challenge "fraudulent conduct" sentence confirmed verbatim.

29-6.1 grants open-ended structural discretion subject to three express limits (10-year cap, 5-year termination option, 15-year EV exception) and expressly authorizes "contract opener clauses for any purpose." 23 IAC 1.510 imposes no renewal conditions. No state-agency approval is required.

All load-bearing official URLs re-opened and verbatim-matched, with two URL corrections (29-6.4 .htm canonical replaces broken fulltext.asp URL; 30 ILCS 525 ilcs5.asp?ActID=537 replaces broken ilcs3.asp ChapterID=7 URL). No substantive rule changed.

IASB Update re-verified and corroborates the 10-year-before-rebid framing. Stage 1's stale-ISBE-FAQ observation is retained as illustrative of practical variance (the FAQ is non-authority); the ISBE PDF was accessible on 2026-06-23 but its text could not be re-rendered text-side on the adversarial pass — this does not affect the statewide rule.
Re-opened 105 ILCS 5/14-7.02 on official ilga.gov; no separate special-ed procurement/term/rebid/renewal rule. 29-6.4 expressly extends the same rebid trigger to special-education cooperatives.

verificationNotes:
**SOURCE-VALIDATION REVISIT.** Each load-bearing primary citation behind the Max-Term / Rebid / Renewal answers was re-opened on its official source on 2026-06-23: (1) 105 ILCS 5/29-6.1 (https://www.ilga.gov/documents/legislation/ilcs/documents/010500050K29-6.1.htm) — verified verbatim; source line reads P.A. 103-430, eff. 1-1-24; matches Stage 1's quoted text in full (10-year cap, 5-year termination option for non-EV >5-year contracts, 15-year EV exception conditioned on capital/infrastructure justification, contract-opener-clause permission). (2) 105 ILCS 5/10-20.21 (https://www.ilga.gov/documents/legislation/ilcs/documents/010500050K10-20.21.htm) — verified verbatim; subsection (a)(xvi) carves out pupil-transportation contracts and substitutes a sealed-RFP framework keyed to safety/comfort/stability first and price second; the anti-challenge "fraudulent conduct" sentence was reproduced verbatim by the official source. Source line: P.A. 102-1101, eff. 6-29-22; 103-8, eff. 1-1-24. (3) 105 ILCS 5/29-6.4 (https://www.ilga.gov/documents/legislation/ilcs/documents/010500050K29-6.4.htm) — verified verbatim; the previously-cited fulltext.asp URL returned 404 and was replaced with the .htm canonical official URL; substance unchanged (sealed-bid trigger, lowest-responsible-bidder option, once-every-2-years limit, special-education-cooperative reference, collective-bargaining timing). Source line: P.A. 93-953, eff. 1-1-05.

**Other primary sources re-verified.** 625 ILCS 5/6-106.11 (driver-permit/contractor-conduct overlay); 105 ILCS 5/29-6 (inter-district contracts); 23 Ill. Adm. Code 1.510 (safety/operations only — confirms admin-code silence on procurement); 105 ILCS 5/14-7.02 (no separate special-ed procurement/term rule); Public Act 103-430 (verified to be the amending act that produced the current 29-6.1 text).

**Source-validation revisit conclusion.** Load-bearing primary citation status: VERIFIED for 29-6.1 (Max Term, Renewal Mechanics); VERIFIED for 10-20.21 (Rebid Required for new awards); VERIFIED for 29-6.4 (Rebid Required when district self-operates) at the corrected URL. No load-bearing primary citation was unreachable. Two URL corrections were made (29-6.4 and 30 ILCS 525); both substitutions are official-source-to-official-source and do not change any substantive rule.

**Challenge methodology.** Eight challenge rows (max term; rebid; renewal mechanics; statutory citation; special education; district variance; currentness; applicability boundary) executed. Pattern coverage included: longer-term/renewal-option authority; grandfathering; notwithstanding/may renew/extension/option/successive/multi-year/not exceed/annual termination; sole-source/emergency/cooperative-purchasing/board-waiver/state-agency-approval/negotiated-procurement/small-purchase-threshold exceptions; CPI/fuel/board-approval/annual-appropriations/public-hearing/termination-notice/max-renewal limits; school-specific vs. general-procurement applicability; separate special-ed/IEP/McKinney-Vento/foster-care/homeless/disability transportation provisions; currentness check.

**Negative search paths (no contrary authority found).** (a) 104th-GA amendments to 29-6.1: WebSearch "'105 ILCS 5/29-6.1' amended 104th General Assembly 2025 OR 2026 public act" and "'105 ILCS 5/29-6.1' '104th' amendment OR amended 2025" — no amendment surfaced. (b) Special-ed procurement carve-out in Article 14: WebSearch "Illinois special education transportation contract procurement statute '105 ILCS 5/14' different term limit" + direct WebFetch on 14-7.02 — none found. (c) ISBE updated FAQ superseding stale FAQ #45: WebFetch on the ISBE PDF returned binary content not text-rendered; no superseding text-rendered guidance located on the adversarial pass. The stale-FAQ observation is retained as illustrative practical variance, not authority.

**Source ledger URL corrections (no substantive rule changed).** (1) 105 ILCS 5/29-6.4 — Stage-1 fulltext.asp URL returned 404; corrected to https://www.ilga.gov/documents/legislation/ilcs/documents/010500050K29-6.4.htm. (2) 30 ILCS 525 (Governmental Joint Purchasing Act) — Stage-1 ilcs3.asp ChapterID=7 URL returned 404; corrected to https://www.ilga.gov/legislation/ilcs/ilcs5.asp?ActID=537. (3) Public Act 103-430 effective date confirmed by the source line at 29-6.1 (the PA index page itself does not display an effective-date field text-side).

**Conflict identified.** The only authority-vs.-non-authority conflict in the record is the stale ISBE FAQ (recites pre-P.A. 103-430 language) vs. the current 29-6.1 statute. Resolution: the statute controls; the FAQ is not authority and is recorded as variance, not as substantive contradiction.
